4.1 — Completeness & Duplicates

Zero missing values across all 17 attributes. However 24 exact duplicate rows were identified — removed to prevent overfitting.

0
Missing Values
24
Duplicates
17
Attributes
4.2 — Target Variable Distribution (NObeyesdad)
Imbalance ratio 1.29 — well below 1.5 threshold. Naturally balanced across all 7 classes.
Insufficient Wt
272
Normal Weight
287
Overweight L.I
290
Overweight L.II
290
Obesity Type I
351
Obesity Type II
297
Obesity Type III
324
4.3 — Structural Anomaly Detection

Temporary BMI = Weight/Height² engineered as diagnostic tool only. Confirmed 0 structural errors — no negative values or BMI ≥ 80.

4.4 — Transportation Mode Skewness (MTRANS)

Extreme skew → engineered into transport_active (Walk/Bike) and transport_passive (Auto/Transit/Motorbike).

Public Transit
1,580
Automobile
457
Walking ✓ active
56
Motorbike
11
Bike ✓ active
7
4.5 — Outlier Identification

Boxplot analysis identified extreme values in two features. Winsorization (IQR×1.5) applied in preparation phase.

Age160 outliers
7.6% of 2,111 rows — capped at IQR×1.5 boundary during Winsorization
NCP (Meals/day)580 outliers
27.5% of rows — capped at IQR×1.5 boundary during Winsorization
4.10 — Target Leakage Detection ⚠

Decision Tree audit revealed Weight (44.6%) + Height (23.8%) = 68.4% of predictive power, causing a deceptive 93.98% accuracy. Both deliberately excluded from final training features.

Weight
44.6%
Height
23.8%
Gender
16.0%
Others
15.6%
4.7 — Non-Numeric Features (8 cols)
BinaryGender, FAVC, SMOKE, SCC, family_history → 0/1
OrdinalCAEC, CALC — No=0, Sometimes=1, Frequently=2, Always=3
EngineeredMTRANS → transport_active + transport_passive (binary)
4.8 — Z-Score Standardization (6 features)

Standardized to mean=0, std=1 so SVM/KNN/LR aren't biased by magnitude:

Age FCVC NCP CH2O FAF TUE
00
